---
name: drawing-canvas
description: Freehand drawing canvas with shapes, layers, color picker, undo/redo, and PNG export
metadata:
  contentType: art
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Drawing Canvas

A full-featured drawing canvas module for creating freehand artwork and diagrams.

## Features

- **Freehand Drawing** — Smooth brush strokes with adjustable size using quadratic curve interpolation
- **Shape Tools** — Rectangle, circle, and line tools with live preview while dragging
- **Color Picker** — 12 preset colors plus a custom color input for any hex value
- **Brush Size** — Adjustable from 1px to 100px with a live preview indicator
- **Eraser** — Erase parts of the drawing on any layer
- **Undo/Redo** — Full snapshot-based history with keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y)
- **Clear Canvas** — Wipe all layers with a single action (undoable)
- **Layers** — Add, remove, reorder, and toggle visibility of layers. Each layer shows a live thumbnail
- **Save as PNG** — Export the full composited canvas as a downloadable PNG file
- **Load PNG** — Import a PNG image onto the canvas via file picker

## Usage

```typescript
import { createDrawingCanvas } from './index'

// Create a canvas instance
const canvas = createDrawingCanvas(800, 600)

// Mount it into a DOM container
canvas.mount(document.getElementById('app')!)

// Programmatic control
canvas.dispatch({ type: 'tool-select', tool: 'rectangle' })
canvas.dispatch({ type: 'color-change', color: '#ff0000' })
canvas.dispatch({ type: 'brush-size', size: 8 })
canvas.dispatch({ type: 'undo' })
canvas.dispatch({ type: 'add-layer' })
```

## Server-Side Rendering

```typescript
import { renderDrawingCanvasSSR } from './index'

const html = renderDrawingCanvasSSR(800, 600)
```

## Data Persistence

Drawings can be saved/loaded as JSON to the `data/` directory:

```typescript
import { saveDrawingData, loadDrawingData } from './save-load'

await saveDrawingData(canvas.state, 'my-drawing')
const restored = await loadDrawingData('my-drawing')
```

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Ctrl+Z | Undo |
| Ctrl+Shift+Z | Redo |
| Ctrl+Y | Redo |

input.content:
---
name: drawing-canvas
description: Freehand and shape drawing canvas with layers, undo/redo, and PNG export
metadata:
  contentType: design
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Drawing Canvas

A browser-based drawing canvas that supports freehand drawing, geometric shapes, and multi-layer composition.

## Features

- **Freehand drawing** with adjustable brush size and color
- **Shape tools**: rectangle, circle, and line
- **Eraser tool** that removes content from the active layer
- **Color picker** for selecting stroke color
- **Brush size slider** for fine or thick strokes
- **Undo / Redo** with a 50-step history stack
- **Layers panel** with thumbnails, visibility toggles, add/remove, and reordering
- **Clear canvas** to wipe the active layer
- **Save as PNG** and **load PNG** drawings via server API
- **Real-time compositing** of all visible layers onto a display canvas

## Usage

Start the module server, then open the canvas in a browser. Select a tool from the toolbar, pick a color, adjust brush size, and draw on the canvas. Use the layers panel on the right to manage layers. Drawings persist to the `data/drawings/` directory as PNG files with JSON metadata.

## API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/` | Serves the drawing canvas UI |
| GET | `/api/drawings` | Lists saved drawings |
| POST | `/api/save` | Saves the current canvas as PNG |
| GET | `/api/load/:id` | Loads a saved drawing PNG |
| DELETE | `/api/drawings/:id` | Deletes a saved drawing |

```
└── data/drawings/                  # Persistent storage for saved drawings
```

- **Shape tools** — rectangle, circle, line with live preview on a temp canvas
- **Color picker** and **brush size slider** (1–64px) in the sidebar
- **Eraser** using `destination-out` composite operation
- **Undo/Redo** — 50-step history stack with Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z shortcuts

- **Layers panel** — thumbnails, visibility toggles, add/remove, active selection (max 10 layers)
- **Compositing** — all visible layers merged onto a single display canvas with white background
- **Save as PNG** — `POST /api/save` stores base64 PNG + JSON metadata to `data/drawings/`
- **Load drawings** — `GET /api/load/:id` serves saved PNGs; UI lists available drawings
- **Keyboard shortcuts** — Ctrl+S (save), Ctrl+Z (undo), Ctrl+Shift+Z (redo)

---
name: playlist-manager
description: Create and manage music playlists with playback controls, shuffle, repeat, and drag-to-reorder
metadata:
  contentType: entertainment
  structure: list
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Playlist Manager

A music playlist manager that lets users organize songs into playlists with full playback simulation.

## Features

- **Playlist CRUD** — Create, rename, and delete playlists
- **Song management** — Add songs with title, artist, album, duration, and album art URL
- **Drag-to-reorder** — Reorder songs within a playlist via drag-and-drop
- **Playback controls** — Play, pause, skip forward, skip back with a seekable progress bar
- **Now playing** — Displays current song info with album art
- **Shuffle mode** — Randomize playback order
- **Repeat modes** — Off, repeat-all, repeat-one

## Data

Playlists and songs are persisted as JSON in the `data/` directory.

## Usage

```ts
import { createPlaylistManager } from './index'

const manager = createPlaylistManager()

// Create a playlist
const playlist = manager.createPlaylist('Road Trip')

// Add a song
manager.addSong(playlist.id, {
  title: 'Bohemian Rhapsody',
  artist: 'Queen',
  album: 'A Night at the Opera',
  durationSec: 354,
  albumArtUrl: 'https://example.com/art.jpg',
})

// Playback
manager.play(playlist.id)
manager.skip()
manager.toggleShuffle()
manager.setRepeat('all')

// Render UI
const html = manager.render()
```

export type Song = {
  id: string
  title: string
  artist: string
  album: string
  durationSec: number
  albumArtUrl: string
}

export type Playlist = {
  id: string
  name: string
  songs: Song[]
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
}

export type RepeatMode = 'off' | 'all' | 'one'

export type PlaybackState = {
  currentPlaylistId: string | null
  currentSongIndex: number
  isPlaying: boolean
  progressSec: number
  shuffle: boolean
  repeat: RepeatMode
  shuffleOrder: number[]
}

export type StoreData = {
  playlists: Playlist[]
  playback: PlaybackState
}

import type { Song, RepeatMode } from './types'
import * as store from './store'
import * as playback from './playback'
import { renderPage } from './render'

export type PlaylistManager = {
  createPlaylist: (name: string) => ReturnType<typeof store.createPlaylist>
  deletePlaylist: (id: string) => ReturnType<typeof store.deletePlaylist>
  renamePlaylist: (id: string, name: string) => ReturnType<typeof store.renamePlaylist>
  getPlaylist: (id: string) => ReturnType<typeof store.getPlaylist>
  getAllPlaylists: () => ReturnType<typeof store.getAllPlaylists>
  addSong: (playlistId: string, song: Omit<Song, 'id'>) => ReturnType<typeof store.addSong>
  removeSong: (playlistId: string, songId: string) => ReturnType<typeof store.removeSong>
  reorderSong: (playlistId: string, from: number, to: number) => ReturnType<typeof store.reorderSong>
  play: (playlistId?: string, songIndex?: number) => ReturnType<typeof playback.play>
  pause: () => ReturnType<typeof playback.pause>
  togglePlayPause: () => ReturnType<typeof playback.togglePlayPause>
  skip: () => ReturnType<typeof playback.skip>
  previous: () => ReturnType<typeof playback.previous>
  seek: (positionSec: number) => ReturnType<typeof playback.seek>
  toggleShuffle: () => ReturnType<typeof playback.toggleShuffle>
  setRepeat: (mode: RepeatMode) => ReturnType<typeof playback.setRepeat>
  cycleRepeat: () => ReturnType<typeof playback.cycleRepeat>
  getCurrentSong: () => ReturnType<typeof playback.getCurrentSong>
  render: (activePlaylistId?: string) => ReturnType<typeof renderPage>
}

export const createPlaylistManager = (): PlaylistManager => ({
  createPlaylist: store.createPlaylist,
  deletePlaylist: store.deletePlaylist,
  renamePlaylist: store.renamePlaylist,
  getPlaylist: store.getPlaylist,
  getAllPlaylists: store.getAllPlaylists,
  addSong: store.addSong,
  removeSong: store.removeSong,
  reorderSong: store.reorderSong,
  play: playback.play,
  pause: playback.pause,
  togglePlayPause: playback.togglePlayPause,
  skip: playback.skip,
  previous: playback.previous,
  seek: playback.seek,
  toggleShuffle: playback.toggleShuffle,
  setRepeat: playback.setRepeat,
  cycleRepeat: playback.cycleRepeat,
  getCurrentSong: playback.getCurrentSong,
  render: renderPage,
})

export { store, playback, renderPage }
export type { Song, Playlist, PlaybackState, RepeatMode } from './types'

---
name: playlist-manager
description: Music playlist manager with playback controls, drag-reorder, shuffle, and repeat
metadata:

# Playlist Manager

A full-featured music playlist manager module.

## Features

- **Create & manage playlists** — name, describe, and organize multiple playlists
- **Song management** — add songs with title, artist, album, duration, and album art URL
- **Drag-to-reorder** — reorder songs within a playlist via drag and drop
- **Playback controls** — play, pause, skip forward, skip back with a seekable progress bar
- **Now playing** — displays current song info with album art
- **Shuffle mode** — randomize playback order without altering the playlist
- **Repeat modes** — repeat all, repeat one, or no repeat

## Usage

1. Create a new playlist via the create-playlist form
2. Add songs to a playlist by providing title, artist, album, duration, and optional album art URL
3. Drag songs to reorder them in the playlist
4. Use playback controls to play/pause/skip tracks
5. Toggle shuffle and repeat modes from the transport bar
6. View now-playing info with album art in the player panel

## Data

Playlists and songs are persisted as JSON in the `data/` directory.

---
name: playlist-manager
description: Music playlist manager with playback controls, drag-to-reorder, shuffle, and repeat
metadata:
  contentType: entertainment
  structure: list
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Playlist Manager

A music playlist manager that lets users organize and control their music.

## Features

- **Create & manage playlists** — name, describe, and manage multiple playlists
- **Add songs** — each song has title, artist, album, duration, and album art URL
- **Drag-to-reorder** — reorder songs within a playlist via drag-and-drop
- **Playback controls** — play, pause, skip forward, skip back with a progress bar
- **Now playing** — displays current song info with album art
- **Shuffle mode** — randomize playback order
- **Repeat mode** — loop the current playlist or single track

## Usage

```ts
import { createPlaylist, addSong, getPlaylists } from './playlist'
import { createPlaybackState, play, pause, skip, previous, toggleShuffle, toggleRepeat } from './playback'
import { renderApp } from './ui'
```

## Data Model

Songs are stored with: `id`, `title`, `artist`, `album`, `durationSeconds`, `albumArtUrl`.
Playlists contain an ordered array of songs plus metadata (`id`, `name`, `description`, `createdAt`).
Playback state tracks the current playlist, song index, progress, shuffle, and repeat mode.

export type Song = {
  id: string
  title: string
  artist: string
  album: string
  durationSeconds: number
  albumArtUrl: string
}

export type Playlist = {
  id: string
  name: string
  description: string
  songs: Song[]
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
}

export type RepeatMode = 'off' | 'all' | 'one'

export type PlaybackState = {
  currentPlaylistId: string | null
  currentSongIndex: number
  isPlaying: boolean
  progressSeconds: number
  shuffle: boolean
  repeat: RepeatMode
  shuffleOrder: number[]
}

export type PlaylistStore = {
  playlists: Playlist[]
}

---
name: statistics-calculator
description: Calculate descriptive statistics and render visualizations for numeric datasets
metadata:
  contentType: data-viz
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Statistics Calculator

Accepts numeric datasets as comma-separated values or JSON arrays and computes
descriptive statistics including mean, median, mode, standard deviation, variance,
min, max, range, quartiles (Q1, Q3), and interquartile range (IQR).

## Features

- **Parse** datasets from CSV strings (`1,2,3,4`) or JSON arrays (`[1,2,3,4]`).
- **Named datasets** — store multiple datasets by name for side-by-side comparison.
- **Summary table** — all statistics rendered in a comparison table.
- **Box plot** — SVG box-and-whisker plot for each dataset.
- **Histogram** — SVG frequency histogram for each dataset.

## Usage

```ts
import { parseDataset } from './parser'
import { computeStats } from './stats'
import { renderDashboard } from './render'

const data = parseDataset('10,20,30,40,50')
const stats = computeStats(data)
const html = renderDashboard({ myData: data })
```

### Adding a named dataset

```ts
import { addDataset, loadDatasets } from './store'

await addDataset('experiment-a', '3,7,8,12,15,18,22')
const all = await loadDatasets()
```

# Statistics Calculator

Accepts one or more named datasets as comma-separated numbers or JSON arrays.
Computes descriptive statistics and renders visual comparisons.

## Supported Statistics

- **Central tendency**: mean, median, mode
- **Spread**: standard deviation, variance, range, IQR
- **Extremes**: min, max
- **Quartiles**: Q1 (25th percentile), Q3 (75th percentile)

## Input Formats

- Comma-separated: `1, 2, 3, 4, 5`
- JSON array: `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]`

## Features

- Parse and validate numeric datasets
- Calculate all descriptive statistics at once
- Render an SVG box plot for each dataset
- Render an SVG histogram for each dataset
- Compare multiple named datasets side by side in a summary table
- Persist datasets to `data/datasets.json`

## Usage

1. **Add a dataset**: provide a name and data (CSV or JSON array)
2. **View statistics**: see computed stats for any dataset
3. **Compare**: view a summary table with all statistics across datasets
4. **Visualize**: render box plot and histogram SVGs

---
name: statistics-calculator
description: Calculate descriptive statistics and render box plots and histograms for numeric datasets
metadata:
  contentType: data-viz
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Statistics Calculator

A module for computing descriptive statistics and rendering data visualizations from numeric datasets.

## Input Formats

- **Comma-separated values**: `"1, 2, 3, 4, 5"`
- **JSON array**: `"[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]"`
- **Programmatic**: `number[]`

## Computed Statistics

For each dataset the following are calculated:

| Statistic | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Count | Number of data points |
| Mean | Arithmetic average |
| Median | Middle value |
| Mode | Most frequently occurring value(s) |
| Standard Deviation | Population standard deviation |
| Variance | Population variance |
| Min / Max | Smallest and largest values |
| Range | Max minus Min |
| Q1 / Q3 | First and third quartiles (linear interpolation) |
| IQR | Interquartile range (Q3 - Q1) |

## Visualizations

- **Box Plot** — Displays median, quartiles, whiskers, and outliers for each dataset side by side.
- **Histogram** — Grouped bar chart showing frequency distributions across 12 bins.

## Usage

### Inline Analysis

```ts
import { analyzeInline } from './index.ts'

const result = analyzeInline(
  { name: 'Group A', data: '10, 20, 30, 40, 50' },
  { name: 'Group B', data: [15, 25, 35, 45, 55] },
)

// result.html — full HTML page with table + charts
// result.datasets — array of DatasetStats objects
```

### Persistent Datasets

```ts
import { addDataset, analyzeStored } from './index.ts'

await addDataset('Sales Q1', '120, 135, 150, 142, 160')
await addDataset('Sales Q2', '130, 145, 155, 148, 170')

const result = await analyzeStored()
```

### HTTP Server

```ts
import { serve } from './index.ts'
serve(3000)
```

**Endpoints:**

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/` | Full HTML report of stored datasets |
| POST | `/api/analyze` | Analyze datasets inline (body: `{ datasets: [...] }`) |
| POST | `/api/dataset` | Add a named dataset (body: `{ name, data }`) |
| DELETE | `/api/dataset/:name` | Remove a named dataset |
| GET | `/api/datasets` | List stored dataset names |

## Comparing Datasets

Pass multiple datasets to see a side-by-side summary table with all statistics, overlaid box plots, and grouped histograms. Up to 8 datasets can be compared with distinct colors.

---
name: physics-simulator
description: Interactive Newton's laws simulator with pendulum, projectile, and spring-mass systems
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: collection
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Physics Simulator

An interactive collection of three Newton's laws simulations rendered with real-time SVG visualization.

## Simulations

### 1. Simple Pendulum
Demonstrates oscillatory motion governed by gravity. The pendulum bob swings under the restoring force F = -mg sin(theta).

- **Adjustable parameters**: pendulum length (L), gravitational acceleration (g)
- **Displayed values**: angular position (theta), angular velocity (omega), tangential acceleration

### 2. Projectile Motion
Simulates a launched projectile under uniform gravitational acceleration. Traces the parabolic trajectory path.

- **Adjustable parameters**: launch angle (alpha), initial velocity (v0)
- **Displayed values**: x/y position, velocity components (vx, vy), acceleration (g downward)

### 3. Spring-Mass System
Models simple harmonic motion of a mass on a horizontal spring. F = -kx (Hooke's law).

- **Adjustable parameters**: spring constant (k), mass (m)
- **Displayed values**: displacement (x), velocity (v), acceleration (a = -kx/m)

## Controls

- **Play / Pause**: start or pause the simulation
- **Reset**: return the simulation to its initial conditions
- **Speed**: adjust simulation playback speed (0.25x to 4x)

## Usage

```ts
import { createSimulator } from './src/index'

const sim = createSimulator('pendulum', { length: 1.5, gravity: 9.81 })
sim.play()
```

---
name: physics-simulator
description: Interactive Newton's laws simulator with pendulum, projectile, and spring-mass systems
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: steps
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Physics Simulator

An interactive simulator demonstrating Newton's laws of motion through three classic physics experiments.

## Simulations

### 1. Simple Pendulum
Demonstrates simple harmonic motion with a swinging pendulum.
- **Adjustable parameters:** pendulum length (0.1–3.0 m), gravity (1–20 m/s²)
- **Displays:** angular position (θ), angular velocity (ω), angular acceleration (α), period

### 2. Projectile Motion
Simulates trajectory of a projectile under gravity.
- **Adjustable parameters:** launch angle (0–90°), initial velocity (1–100 m/s), gravity (1–20 m/s²)
- **Displays:** x/y position, velocity components (vx, vy), time of flight, range, max height

### 3. Spring-Mass System
Models a mass on a horizontal spring exhibiting oscillation.
- **Adjustable parameters:** spring constant k (0.1–100 N/m), mass (0.1–50 kg), damping coefficient
- **Displays:** displacement, velocity, acceleration, potential/kinetic energy

## Controls
- **Play/Pause** — start or pause the simulation
- **Reset** — return to initial conditions
- **Speed** — adjust simulation speed (0.25×–4×)

## Usage
```ts
import { createSimulator } from './src/index'

const sim = createSimulator({ type: 'pendulum', length: 1.5, gravity: 9.81 })
sim.play()
```

input.content:
export type SimulationType = 'pendulum' | 'projectile' | 'springMass'

export type PlaybackState = 'playing' | 'paused' | 'stopped'

export type Vec2 = {
  x: number
  y: number
}

export type PendulumParams = {
  length: number       // meters (0.1–3.0)
  gravity: number      // m/s² (1–20)
  initialAngle: number // radians
  damping: number      // coefficient (0–1)
}

export type PendulumState = {
  angle: number            // θ in radians
  angularVelocity: number  // ω in rad/s
  angularAcceleration: number // α in rad/s²
  period: number           // T in seconds
  position: Vec2           // bob position in meters
  time: number
}

export type ProjectileParams = {
  angle: number          // degrees (0–90)
  initialVelocity: number // m/s (1–100)
  gravity: number        // m/s² (1–20)
  height: number         // initial height in meters
}

export type ProjectileState = {
  position: Vec2
  velocity: Vec2
  acceleration: Vec2
  time: number
  maxHeight: number
  range: number
  landed: boolean
  trajectory: Vec2[] // path history
}

export type SpringMassParams = {
  springConstant: number      // k in N/m (0.1–100)
  mass: number                // kg (0.1–50)
  damping: number             // coefficient (0–2)
  initialDisplacement: number // meters
}

export type SpringMassState = {
  displacement: number   // x in meters
  velocity: number       // m/s
  acceleration: number   // m/s²
  potentialEnergy: number // J
  kineticEnergy: number   // J
  totalEnergy: number     // J
  time: number
}

export type SimulationConfig = {
  speed: number // 0.25–4.0
  dt: number    // time step in seconds
}

export type SimulatorControls = {
  play: () => void
  pause: () => void
  reset: () => void
  setSpeed: (speed: number) => void
  getState: () => PendulumState | ProjectileState | SpringMassState
  isPlaying: () => boolean
}

input.content:
---
name: physics-simulator
description: Interactive Newton's laws simulator with pendulum, projectile, and spring-mass systems
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: steps
  boundary: all
  scale: "3"
---

# Physics Simulator

An interactive module for simulating three fundamental physics systems governed by Newton's laws of motion.

## Simulations

### 1. Simple Pendulum
Demonstrates oscillatory motion under gravity. The pendulum swings according to:
- Angular acceleration: `a = -(g/L) * sin(theta)`
- Adjustable parameters: pendulum length (L), gravitational acceleration (g)
- Displays: angular position, angular velocity, angular acceleration, and x/y coordinates

### 2. Projectile Motion
Simulates a projectile launched at an angle, tracing its parabolic trajectory:
- Horizontal: `x = v0 * cos(angle) * t`
- Vertical: `y = v0 * sin(angle) * t - 0.5 * g * t^2`
- Adjustable parameters: launch angle, initial velocity
- Displays: position (x, y), velocity (vx, vy), acceleration, and trajectory path

### 3. Spring-Mass System
Models simple harmonic motion of a mass attached to a spring:
- Displacement: `x = A * cos(omega * t)` where `omega = sqrt(k/m)`
- Adjustable parameters: spring constant (k), mass (m)
- Displays: displacement, velocity, acceleration, and energy (kinetic + potential)

## Controls
- **Play/Pause**: Start or pause the simulation
- **Reset**: Return to initial conditions
- **Speed**: Adjust simulation speed from 0.25x to 4x

## Usage
Select a simulation tab, adjust parameters using sliders, and press Play to observe the physics in real-time. All values update live during simulation.

---
name: periodic-table
description: Interactive periodic table explorer with all 118 elements
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: collection
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Periodic Table Explorer

An interactive periodic table displaying all 118 elements in the standard 18-column layout.

## Features

- **Full periodic table** with all 118 elements positioned in the standard layout including lanthanide and actinide series
- **Color-coded categories**: alkali metals, alkaline earth metals, transition metals, post-transition metals, metalloids, nonmetals, halogens, noble gases, lanthanides, and actinides
- **Element cells** showing atomic number, symbol, name, and atomic mass
- **Detail panel** on element click showing electron configuration, oxidation states, melting/boiling points, discovery year, and description
- **Search bar** to find elements by name, symbol, or atomic number
- **Category filter** to highlight or isolate elements by classification

## Usage

Render the periodic table with optional filters:

```typescript
import { renderPeriodicTable } from './src/index';

// Full table
const html = renderPeriodicTable();

// With a selected element
const html = renderPeriodicTable({ selectedElement: 26 });

// With search query
const html = renderPeriodicTable({ query: 'iron' });

// With category filter
const html = renderPeriodicTable({ categories: ['noble-gas', 'halogen'] });
```

input.content:
---
name: periodic-table
description: Interactive periodic table explorer with all 118 elements, color-coded categories, search, and detail panels
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: collection
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Periodic Table Explorer

An interactive periodic table of elements that displays all 118 known elements in the standard 18-column layout.

## Features

- **Full 18-column layout** with all 118 elements positioned correctly, including lanthanides and actinides
- **Color-coded categories**: alkali metals, alkaline earth metals, transition metals, post-transition metals, metalloids, nonmetals, halogens, noble gases, lanthanides, actinides, and unknown properties
- **Element cells** show atomic number, symbol, name, and atomic mass
- **Detail panel** opens on click showing electron configuration, oxidation states, melting/boiling points, discovery year, and description
- **Search bar** for finding elements by name, symbol, or atomic number
- **Category filter** to highlight or isolate element groups

## Usage

Render the periodic table as a full-page interactive view. Click any element to see detailed properties. Use the search bar to find specific elements, or use the category dropdown to filter by element group.

export type ElementCategory =
  | 'alkali-metal'
  | 'alkaline-earth-metal'
  | 'transition-metal'
  | 'post-transition-metal'
  | 'metalloid'
  | 'nonmetal'
  | 'halogen'
  | 'noble-gas'
  | 'lanthanide'
  | 'actinide'
  | 'unknown'

export type Element = {
  atomicNumber: number
  symbol: string
  name: string
  atomicMass: string
  category: ElementCategory
  row: number
  col: number
  electronConfiguration: string
  oxidationStates: string
  meltingPoint: string
  boilingPoint: string
  discoveryYear: string
  description: string
}

export type CategoryMeta = {
  label: string
  color: string
  textColor: string
}

export const CATEGORY_META: Record<ElementCategory, CategoryMeta> = {
  'alkali-metal': { label: 'Alkali Metal', color: '#ff6b6b', textColor: '#fff' },
  'alkaline-earth-metal': { label: 'Alkaline Earth Metal', color: '#ffa94d', textColor: '#000' },
  'transition-metal': { label: 'Transition Metal', color: '#ffd43b', textColor: '#000' },
  'post-transition-metal': { label: 'Post-Transition Metal', color: '#69db7c', textColor: '#000' },
  'metalloid': { label: 'Metalloid', color: '#38d9a9', textColor: '#000' },
  'nonmetal': { label: 'Nonmetal', color: '#4dabf7', textColor: '#000' },
  'halogen': { label: 'Halogen', color: '#748ffc', textColor: '#fff' },
  'noble-gas': { label: 'Noble Gas', color: '#da77f2', textColor: '#fff' },
  'lanthanide': { label: 'Lanthanide', color: '#e599f7', textColor: '#000' },
  'actinide': { label: 'Actinide', color: '#faa2c1', textColor: '#000' },
  'unknown': { label: 'Unknown Properties', color: '#adb5bd', textColor: '#000' },
}

import type { ElementCategory } from './types.ts'
import { CATEGORY_META } from './types.ts'

export const styles = {
  container: {
    fontFamily: "'Segoe UI', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif",
    maxWidth: '1400px',
    margin: '0 auto',
    padding: '20px',
    backgroundColor: '#1a1a2e',
    color: '#e0e0e0',
    minHeight: '100vh',
    boxSizing: 'border-box' as const,
  },
  header: {
    textAlign: 'center' as const,
    marginBottom: '24px',
  },
  title: {
    fontSize: '28px',
    fontWeight: '700',
    color: '#ffffff',
    margin: '0 0 16px 0',
    letterSpacing: '1px',
  },
  controls: {
    display: 'flex',
    gap: '12px',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    flexWrap: 'wrap' as const,
    marginBottom: '24px',
  },
  searchInput: {
    padding: '10px 16px',
    fontSize: '14px',
    borderRadius: '8px',
    border: '1px solid #3a3a5c',
    backgroundColor: '#16213e',
    color: '#e0e0e0',
    width: '260px',
    outline: 'none',
  },
  filterSelect: {
    padding: '10px 16px',
    fontSize: '14px',
    borderRadius: '8px',
    border: '1px solid #3a3a5c',
    backgroundColor: '#16213e',
    color: '#e0e0e0',
    outline: 'none',
    cursor: 'pointer',
  },
  tableWrapper: {
    overflowX: 'auto' as const,
    paddingBottom: '16px',
  },
  table: {
    display: 'grid',
    gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(18, minmax(68px, 1fr))',
    gap: '2px',
    marginBottom: '12px',
    minWidth: '1240px',
  },
  lanthanideActinideSection: {
    display: 'grid',
    gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(18, minmax(68px, 1fr))',
    gap: '2px',
    marginTop: '12px',
    minWidth: '1240px',
  },
  elementCell: (category: ElementCategory, dimmed: boolean) => ({
    backgroundColor: dimmed
      ? `${CATEGORY_META[category].color}22`
      : CATEGORY_META[category].color,
    color: dimmed ? '#666' : CATEGORY_META[category].textColor,
    borderRadius: '4px',
    padding: '4px',
    cursor: 'pointer',
    textAlign: 'center' as const,
    transition: 'transform 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s, opacity 0.15s',
    border: '1px solid transparent',
    minHeight: '72px',
    display: 'flex',
    flexDirection: 'column' as const,
    justifyContent: 'center',
    alignItems: 'center',
    position: 'relative' as const,
    opacity: dimmed ? 0.4 : 1,
  }),
  elementCellHover: {
    transform: 'scale(1.1)',
    boxShadow: '0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)',
    zIndex: '10',
    border: '1px solid #fff',
  },
  atomicNumber: {
    fontSize: '10px',
    lineHeight: '1',
    opacity: '0.85',
  },
  symbol: {
    fontSize: '20px',
    fontWeight: '700',
    lineHeight: '1.2',
  },
  elementName: {
    fontSize: '8px',
    lineHeight: '1',
    whiteSpace: 'nowrap' as const,
    overflow: 'hidden',
    textOverflow: 'ellipsis',
    maxWidth: '100%',
  },
  atomicMass: {
    fontSize: '8px',
    lineHeight: '1',
    opacity: '0.75',
  },
  emptyCell: {
    visibility: 'hidden' as const,
  },
  legend: {
    display: 'flex',
    flexWrap: 'wrap' as const,
    gap: '8px',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    marginTop: '16px',
    marginBottom: '16px',
  },
  legendItem: (category: ElementCategory) => ({
    display: 'flex',
    alignItems: 'center',
    gap: '4px',
    fontSize: '11px',
    color: '#ccc',
  }),
  legendSwatch: (category: ElementCategory) => ({
    width: '12px',
    height: '12px',
    borderRadius: '2px',
    backgroundColor: CATEGORY_META[category].color,
    display: 'inline-block',
  }),
  detailOverlay: {
    position: 'fixed' as const,
    top: '0',
    left: '0',
    right: '0',
    bottom: '0',
    backgroundColor: 'rgba(0,0,0,0.7)',
    display: 'flex',
    alignItems: 'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    zIndex: '1000',
  },
  detailPanel: (category: ElementCategory) => ({
    backgroundColor: '#1a1a2e',
    border: `2px solid ${CATEGORY_META[category].color}`,
    borderRadius: '16px',
    padding: '32px',
    maxWidth: '480px',
    width: '90%',
    maxHeight: '85vh',
    overflowY: 'auto' as const,
    position: 'relative' as const,
    boxShadow: `0 0 40px ${CATEGORY_META[category].color}44`,
  }),
  detailCloseBtn: {
    position: 'absolute' as const,
    top: '12px',
    right: '16px',
    background: 'none',
    border: 'none',
    color: '#aaa',
    fontSize: '24px',
    cursor: 'pointer',
    lineHeight: '1',
    padding: '4px 8px',
  },
  detailHeader: (category: ElementCategory) => ({
    display: 'flex',
    alignItems: 'center',
    gap: '20px',
    marginBottom: '24px',
  }),
  detailSymbolBox: (category: ElementCategory) => ({
    width: '90px',
    height: '90px',
    backgroundColor: CATEGORY_META[category].color,
    color: CATEGORY_META[category].textColor,
    borderRadius: '12px',
    display: 'flex',
    flexDirection: 'column' as const,
    alignItems: 'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    flexShrink: 0,
  }),
  detailSymbol: {
    fontSize: '36px',
    fontWeight: '700',
    lineHeight: '1',
  },
  detailSmallNum: {
    fontSize: '12px',
    opacity: '0.85',
  },
  detailNameSection: {
    flex: '1',
  },
  detailName: {
    fontSize: '24px',
    fontWeight: '700',
    color: '#fff',
    margin: '0 0 4px 0',
  },
  detailCategory: (category: ElementCategory) => ({
    fontSize: '13px',
    color: CATEGORY_META[category].color,
    fontWeight: '500',
  }),
  detailMass: {
    fontSize: '14px',
    color: '#aaa',
    marginTop: '4px',
  },
  detailGrid: {
    display: 'grid',
    gridTemplateColumns: '1fr 1fr',
    gap: '16px',
    marginBottom: '20px',
  },
  detailField: {
    padding: '0',
  },
  detailLabel: {
    fontSize: '11px',
    color: '#888',
    textTransform: 'uppercase' as const,
    letterSpacing: '0.5px',
    marginBottom: '4px',
  },
  detailValue: {
    fontSize: '14px',
    color: '#e0e0e0',
  },
  detailDescription: {
    fontSize: '14px',
    lineHeight: '1.6',
    color: '#bbb',
    borderTop: '1px solid #333',
    paddingTop: '16px',
    marginTop: '8px',
  },
  labelArrow: {
    gridColumn: '3',
    display: 'flex',
    alignItems: 'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    color: '#666',
    fontSize: '12px',
  },
}

All 118 elements present. The module is complete with this structure:

```
periodic-table/
├── package.json              # modnet config (science, collection, filter+sort)
├── skills/periodic-table/
│   └── SKILL.md              # Seed skill with frontmatter
├── data/
│   └── elements.json         # All 118 elements with full properties
└── src/
    ├── types.ts              # Element & category types, color mappings
    ├── styles.ts             # Full styling (dark theme, category colors)
    ├── data.ts               # Element data loader (Bun.file)
    ├── detail-panel.ts       # Click-to-open detail overlay
    ├── periodic-table.ts     # Main 18-column grid layout with search/filter
    └── index.ts              # Entry point, SSR rendering, app state
```

**Key features:**
- **18-column grid layout** with correct element positions, lanthanides/actinides in separate rows below with placeholder cells in the main grid
- **11 category colors**: alkali metals (red), noble gases (purple), transition metals (yellow), halogens (indigo), metalloids (teal), nonmetals (blue), etc.
- **Element cells** display atomic number, symbol, name, and atomic mass
- **Detail panel** overlay on click shows electron configuration, oxidation states, melting/boiling points, discovery year, and description
- **Search bar** filters by name, symbol, or atomic number; non-matching elements are dimmed
- **Category dropdown** to filter by element group
- Uses plaited `h()` templates with `createSSR()` for server-side rendering
- Dark theme (`#1a1a2e`) with category-colored cells

- **`src/elements.ts`** — All 118 elements with atomic number, symbol, name, mass, grid position, electron configuration, oxidation states, melting/boiling points, discovery year, and description
- **`src/render.ts`** — Full UI rendering using plaited `h()` templates:
  - 18-column grid layout with rows 1-7 for the main table, row 9 for lanthanides (57-71), row 10 for actinides (89-103)
  - Color-coded cells by category
  - Search input and category filter dropdown
  - Slide-in detail panel with element properties
  - Category legend
- **`src/index.ts`** — Entry point with `render()`, `selectElement()`, `closeDetail()`, `search()`, `filterByCategory()`, and lookup helpers
- **`data/state.json`** — Persistent state for selected element, search query, and active filter

input.content:
---
name: periodic-table
description: Interactive periodic table explorer with all 118 elements, color-coded categories, search, filtering, and detailed element info
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: collection
  boundary: all
  scale: "3"
---

# Periodic Table Explorer

An interactive periodic table module displaying all 118 chemical elements in the standard 18-column layout.

## Features

- **Full Table Layout**: Standard 18-column periodic table with lanthanides and actinides displayed separately below
- **Color-Coded Categories**: Elements are color-coded by category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, halogens, etc.)
- **Element Cells**: Each cell shows atomic number, symbol, element name, and atomic mass
- **Detail Panel**: Click any element to view detailed information including:
  - Electron configuration
  - Common oxidation states
  - Melting and boiling points
  - Year of discovery
  - Brief description of the element
- **Search**: Search elements by name, symbol, or atomic number
- **Category Filter**: Filter the table to highlight or isolate elements by category

## Usage

Render the periodic table as a full-page interactive application. Use the search bar to find specific elements or the category dropdown to filter by element group. Click any element cell to open its detail panel.

export type ElementCategory =
  | 'alkali-metal'
  | 'alkaline-earth-metal'
  | 'transition-metal'
  | 'post-transition-metal'
  | 'metalloid'
  | 'reactive-nonmetal'
  | 'noble-gas'
  | 'lanthanide'
  | 'actinide'
  | 'unknown'

export type Element = {
  number: number
  symbol: string
  name: string
  mass: string
  category: ElementCategory
  row: number
  col: number
  electronConfig: string
  oxidationStates: string
  meltingPoint: string
  boilingPoint: string
  discoveryYear: string
  description: string
}

export type CategoryInfo = {
  label: string
  color: string
  textColor: string
}

export type AppState = {
  selectedElement: number | null
  searchQuery: string
  categoryFilter: ElementCategory | 'all'
}

import type { ElementCategory, CategoryInfo } from './types'

export const CATEGORIES: Record<ElementCategory, CategoryInfo> = {
  'alkali-metal': { label: 'Alkali Metals', color: '#ff6961', textColor: '#000' },
  'alkaline-earth-metal': { label: 'Alkaline Earth Metals', color: '#f5c242', textColor: '#000' },
  'transition-metal': { label: 'Transition Metals', color: '#f4c7a3', textColor: '#000' },
  'post-transition-metal': { label: 'Post-Transition Metals', color: '#a8d8b9', textColor: '#000' },
  'metalloid': { label: 'Metalloids', color: '#c4b7a6', textColor: '#000' },
  'reactive-nonmetal': { label: 'Reactive Nonmetals', color: '#7bc8f6', textColor: '#000' },
  'noble-gas': { label: 'Noble Gases', color: '#c4a3d4', textColor: '#000' },
  'lanthanide': { label: 'Lanthanides', color: '#f2b5d4', textColor: '#000' },
  'actinide': { label: 'Actinides', color: '#d4a0c0', textColor: '#000' },
  'unknown': { label: 'Unknown Properties', color: '#d9d9d9', textColor: '#000' },
}

input.description: List all files in the module

trajectory.content:
All 118 elements are present. The module is complete with:

- **`package.json`** — Module metadata with `modnet` config (contentType: `science`, structure: `collection`, mechanics: `filter`/`sort`)
- **`skills/periodic-table/SKILL.md`** — Seed skill with frontmatter and usage docs
- **`src/types.ts`** — Type definitions for `Element`, `ElementCategory`, `CategoryInfo`, `AppState`
- **`src/categories.ts`** — 10 element categories with color codes (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, lanthanides, actinides, etc.)
- **`src/elements.ts`** — All 118 elements with atomic number, symbol, name, mass, grid position, electron configuration, oxidation states, melting/boiling points, discovery year, and description
- **`src/render.ts`** — Full UI rendering using plaited `h()` templates:
  - 18-column grid layout with rows 1-7 for the main table, row 9 for lanthanides (57-71), row 10 for actinides (89-103)
  - Color-coded cells by category
  - Search input and category filter dropdown
  - Slide-in detail panel with element properties
  - Category legend
- **`src/index.ts`** — Entry point with `render()`, `selectElement()`, `closeDetail()`, `search()`, `filterByCategory()`, and lookup helpers
- **`data/state.json`** — Persistent state for selected element, search query, and active filter
